title: EKP Phase Review Output (Red Team)
description: Adversarial review of one phase's code by an isolated Claude agent. Default to passed=false unless evidence of compliance is conclusive. Strict-mode: every object has additionalProperties=false and every property is required (use defaults for not-applicable).

passed.description: True iff: (a) all phase gates_satisfied are met with evidence, (b) verification commands exit 0, (c) no Critical/High findings, (d) no scope violations.

findings.description: Specific defects found. Empty array allowed only if passed=true.

category.description: gate_unmet=phase gate not satisfied; scope_violation=changed file outside scope_globs; regression=broke a previously-passing test.

evidence.description: Concrete proof: file:line excerpt, test output, command output.

command.description: Exact shell command run from phase.verification.how_to_test.

output_summary.description: Truncated stdout/stderr, last 50 lines or relevant excerpt.

regression_check.description: If prior phases existed, re-run their verification commands.

prior_phases_re_run.description: Phase IDs whose verification was re-run. Use [] if this is the first phase.

all_prior_still_pass.description: True iff all prior_phases_re_run still pass (or true vacuously when array is empty).

scope_audit.description: Files changed in this attempt vs scope_globs.

files_changed.description: Every file the build agent created or modified.

out_of_scope.description: Subset of files_changed that don't match any of the phase's scope_globs. Use [] if all changes were in scope.

recommendation.description: accept=move to next phase; retry=give findings back to build agent; escalate=human intervention needed.

project_memory_used.description: True if this phase successfully read the project memory file (PROJECT_CONTEXT.md) at start; false if missing/unreadable. Required for audit trail.

project_memory_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the project memory file contents as read by this phase. null if the file was missing. Required so post-run audit can confirm all phases used the same memory snapshot.

requirement_doc_alignment.description: verified_aligned = this phase's artifacts were explicitly checked against the requirement_doc path in project memory (the authoritative spec) and they align with it. unavailable = requirement_doc path was missing/unreadable, so final verification against authoritative spec was not possible. The authoritative spec must be the final fallback verification target.
